name: Nanaimo Bars/Crisco
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, cook the Golden Crisco shortening, sugar, cocoa, and vanilla over low heat, stirring until smooth.
2. Add the beaten egg and continue to stir just until the mixture starts to thicken.
3. Remove from heat and stir in the Graham wafer crumbs, coconut, and walnuts. Mix well to ensure the chocolate is evenly distributed throughout the crumbs.
4. Press firmly into a greased 9 x 9-inch (2.5 L) square cake pan and chill for at least half an hour.
Filling:
5. In a small bowl, cream the Golden Crisco shortening, custard powder, milk, and vanilla until blended. Gradually add the sifted icing sugar while beating until smooth, then beat at high speed with an electric mixer until light and creamy, adding a little more milk if needed. Spread evenly over the first layer in the pan.
6. Chill for half an hour until firm.
Top:
7. Heat the chocolate and Golden Crisco shortening on low heat, stirring until smoothly melted. Quickly spread the chocolate evenly over the filling. Chill until firm. Cut into bars. Makes about 30 bars. Freezing: Excellent Hint: If the chocolate becomes too firm for easy spreading, reheat it gently on low heat.
